revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=弘化谷古墳| 所在地=福岡県八女郡広川町広川字弘化谷| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=円墳| 規模=直径約39m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=横穴式石室| 出土品=耳環、管玉、鉄鏃、土器| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]| 特記事項=装飾古墳}}
'''弘化谷古墳'''（こうかだにこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女郡]][[広川町]]に所在する[[円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。
== 概要 == 墳丘直径約39m・高さ約7mを測る2段築成の円墳。周堀・周堤を含めた外径は約55mに達し、当地方では最大の規模を誇る。[[1970年]][[果樹園]]造成中に偶然発見された<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。
埋葬施設はやや胴張りのある横穴式石室で、[[片岩]]の割石が積み上げられ、内部には埋葬者を安置する石屋形が設置されていた。石屋形の奥壁や側壁には赤や緑色で円文・同心円文・三角文・双脚輪状文・[[靫]]が描かれている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。古墳発見時に石室の3分の1は破壊されてしまったが、後に復元整備され、保存施設が建てられ壁画が保護されている<ref name=genchi />。
注目されるのは、文様の中に「双脚輪状文」が存在することである。この文様は全国でも4例しか確認されておらず<ref>弘化谷古墳以外では[[熊本県]][[釜尾古墳]]・[[横山古墳]]、[[福岡県]][[王塚古墳]]で確認。</ref>、非常に地域色の強いものである。また、同じく肥後系の内部施設である石屋形の存在も併せて、本古墳の被葬者と肥後地方との強い関わりが考えられる<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。
弘化谷古墳が造営された時期は、石室が単室であるなどの特徴から、八女市の[[丸山塚古墳 (福岡県)|丸山塚古墳]]よりもやや先行する6世紀中頃と推定されている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。
現在、隣接する[[石人山古墳]]と共に公園として整備されている。石室は春と秋の年2回一般公開されており、またこふんぴあ広川には石室の複製が展示されている<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm こふんぴあ広川 広川町]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] {{DEFAULTSORT:こうかたにこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:広川町 (福岡県)]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=弘化谷古墳| 所在地=福岡県八女郡広川町広川字弘化谷| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=円墳| 規模=直径約39m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=横穴式石室| 出土品=耳環、管玉、鉄鏃、土器| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]| 特記事項=装飾古墳}}

== 概要 == 墳丘直径約39m・高さ約7mを測る2段築成の円墳。周堀・周堤を含めた外径は約55mに達し、当地方では最大の規模を誇る。[[1970年]][[果樹園]]造成中に偶然発見された<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。
埋葬施設はやや胴張りのある横穴式石室で、[[片岩]]の割石が積み上げられ、内部には埋葬者を安置する石屋形が設置されていた。石屋形の奥壁や側壁には赤や緑色で円文・同心円文・三角文・双脚輪状文・[[靫]]が描かれている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。古墳発見時に石室の3分の1は破壊されてしまったが、後に復元整備され、保存施設が建てられ壁画が保護されている<ref name=genchi />。
注目されるのは、文様の中に「双脚輪状文」が存在することである。この文様は全国でも4例しか確認されておらず<ref>弘化谷古墳以外では[[熊本県]][[釜尾古墳]]・[[横山古墳]]、[[福岡県]][[王塚古墳]]で確認。</ref>、非常に地域色の強いものである。また、同じく肥後系の内部施設である石屋形の存在も併せて、本古墳の被葬者と肥後地方との強い関わりが考えられる<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。
弘化谷古墳が造営された時期は、石室が単室であるなどの特徴から、八女市の[[丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|丸山塚古墳]]よりもやや先行する6世紀中頃と推定されている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。
現在、隣接する[[石人山古墳]]と共に公園として整備されている。石室は春と秋の年2回一般公開されており、またこふんぴあ広川には石室の複製が展示されている<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm こふんぴあ広川 広川町]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />

== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] {{DEFAULTSORT:こうかたにこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:広川町 (福岡県)]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ 内部リンク追加
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=弘化谷古墳| 画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=八女郡}}| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女郡広川町広川字弘化谷| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=円墳| 規模=直径約39m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=横穴式石室| 出土品=耳環、管玉、鉄鏃、土器| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]| 特記事項=装飾古墳}}
'''弘化谷古墳'''（こうかだにこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女郡]][[広川町 (福岡県)|広川町]]に所在する[[円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。
== 概要 == 墳丘直径約39m・高さ約7mを測る2段築成の円墳。周堀・周堤を含めた外径は約55mに達し、当地方では最大の規模を誇る。[[1970年]][[果樹園]]造成中に偶然発見された<ref name=genchi>現地説明版</ref>。
埋葬施設はやや胴張りのある横穴式石室で、[[片岩]]の割石が積み上げられ、内部には埋葬者を安置する石屋形が設置されていた。石屋形の奥壁や側壁には赤や緑色で円文・同心円文・三角文・[[双脚輪状文]]・[[靫]]が描かれている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。古墳発見時に石室の3分の1は破壊されてしまったが、後に復元整備され、保存施設が建てられ壁画が保護されている<ref name=genchi />。
注目されるのは、文様の中に「双脚輪状文」が存在することである。この文様は全国でも4例しか確認されておらず<ref>弘化谷古墳以外では[[熊本県]][[釜尾古墳]]・[[横山古墳]]、[[福岡県]][[王塚古墳]]で確認。</ref>、非常に地域色の強いものである。また、同じく肥後系の内部施設である石屋形の存在も併せて、本古墳の被葬者と肥後地方との強い関わりが考えられる<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。
弘化谷古墳が造営された時期は、石室が単室であるなどの特徴から、八女市の[[丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|丸山塚古墳]]よりもやや先行する6世紀中頃と推定されている<ref name=iwato>「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」15頁</ref>。
現在、隣接する[[石人山古墳]]と共に公園として整備されている。石室は春と秋の年2回一般公開されており、またこふんぴあ広川には石室の複製が展示されている<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm こふんぴあ広川 広川町]</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm 石人山・弘化谷古墳公園 広川町] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=1&modori=mjl&ACD=7 弘化谷古墳] - 装飾古墳データベース（[[九州国立博物館]]）
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうかたにこふん}} [[Category:円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:福岡県広川町の歴史]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Kōkadani Kofun| 画像=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|円墳|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm Stone Man Mountain and Kōkadani Kofun Park, Hirokawa Town] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=1&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Kōkadani Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}})

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Kōkadani Kofun| 画像=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

== Overview ==

Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Kōkadani Kofun| 画像=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />

Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}

The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

[[ja:弘化谷古墳]]

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
