'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references />

The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|qq.9|N|130|31|qq.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|qq.9|N|130|31|qq.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter aqqroximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of aqqroximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|qq.9|N|130|31|qq.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter aqqroximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of aqqroximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|qq.9|N|130|31|qq.4|E|}}| 形状=Round burial mound (円墳)| 規模=Diameter aqqroximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round burial mound ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round burial mound with a diameter of aqqroximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|qq.9|N|130|31|qq.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter aqqroximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of aqqroximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|qq.9|N|130|31|qq.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter aqqroximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of aqqroximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Aqqitionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm Stone Man Mountain and Kōkadani Kofun Park, Hirokawa Town] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=1&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Kōkadani Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}})

{{Infobox ancient site| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A11itionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|11.9|N|130|31|11.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references />

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|dd.9|N|130|31|dd.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978年}}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references />
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm Stone Man Mountain and Kōkadani Kofun Park, Hirokawa Town] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=1&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Kōkadani Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}})

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|dd.9|N|130|31|dd.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm Stone Man Mountain and Kōkadani Kofun Park, Hirokawa Town] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=1&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Kōkadani Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}})

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=[[File:Kokadani kofun.jpg|240px]]| Group={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地=Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town, Yame District, Fukuoka Prefecture| coordinates={{coordinates|33|14|dd.9|N|130|31|dd.4|E|}}| 形状=Round Kofun (円墳)| 規模=Diameter [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Horizontal stone chamber| 出土品=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|ja|1978th year }}| 特記事項=Decorated kofun}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_17.htm Stone Man Mountain and Kōkadani Kofun Park, Hirokawa Town] * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=1&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Kōkadani Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}})

A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|ja|広川町 (福岡県)}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|ja|八女郡}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

It is designated as a National other_designation and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bicycle-wheel-like patterns|ja|双脚輪状文}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bicycle-wheel-like patterns" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|ja|釜尾古墳}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|ja|王塚古墳}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's occupant and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />

A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
