'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Yame District, Fukuoka|Yame District, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include [[:en:Kamao Kofun|Kamao Kofun]] and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and [[:en:Ōzuka Kofun|Ōzuka Kofun]] in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include [[:en:Kamao Kofun|Kamao Kofun]] and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and [[:en:Ōzuka Kofun|Ōzuka Kofun]] in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in [[:en:Hirokawa, Fukuoka|Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Yame District, Fukuoka|Yame District, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include [[:en:Kamao Kofun|Kamao Kofun]] and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and [[:en:Ōzuka Kofun|Ōzuka Kofun]] in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>

The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|en|Kamao Kofun|WD=Q24866261|lt=Kamao Kofun}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|en|Ōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11572952|lt=Ōzuka Kofun}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|en|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206740|lt=Hirokawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|en|Yame District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156374|lt=Yame District, Fukuoka}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|en|Kamao Kofun|WD=Q24866261|lt=Kamao Kofun}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|en|Ōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11572952|lt=Ōzuka Kofun}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōkadani Kofun]][[ja:弘化谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōkadani Kofun]][[ja:弘化谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kōkadani Kofun]][[ja:弘化谷古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=File:Kokadani kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.236638888889|N|130.51983333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter approximately 39 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Horizontal stone chamber| Excavated Artifacts=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978}}| Special Notes=Decorated kofun| architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}}}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|en|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206740|lt=Hirokawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|en|Yame District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156374|lt=Yame District, Fukuoka}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|en|Kamao Kofun|WD=Q24866261|lt=Kamao Kofun}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|en|Ōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11572952|lt=Ōzuka Kofun}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōkadani Kofun]][[ja:弘化谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kōkadani Kofun]][[ja:弘化谷古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōkadani Kofun]][[ja:弘化谷古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=File:Kokadani kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740|1=Hirokawa, Fukuoka}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.236638888889|N|130.51983333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter approximately 39 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Horizontal stone chamber| Excavated Artifacts=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}}| Special Notes=Decorated kofun| architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|en|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206740|lt=Hirokawa, Fukuoka|1=Hirokawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|en|Yame District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156374|lt=Yame District, Fukuoka|1=Yame District, Fukuoka}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=Yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=Bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|en|Kamao Kofun|WD=Q24866261|lt=Kamao Kofun|1=Kamao Kofun}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|en|Ōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11572952|lt=Ōzuka Kofun|1=Ōzuka Kofun}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}| name=Kōkadani Kofun| image=File:Kokadani kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Hirokawa|WD=Q1206740|1=Hirokawa, Fukuoka}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.236638888889|N|130.51983333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Round Kofun (円墳)| area=Diameter approximately 39 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Horizontal stone chamber| Excavated Artifacts=Earrings, tubular beads, iron arrowheads, pottery| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1978|en|1978|WD=Q2483|lt=1978|1=1978}}| Special Notes=Decorated kofun| architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}}
'''Kōkadani Kofun''' (弘化谷古墳) is a round Kofun ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in {{ill|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|en|Hirokawa, Fukuoka|WD=Q1206740|lt=Hirokawa, Fukuoka|1=Hirokawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Yame District, Fukuoka|en|Yame District, Fukuoka|WD=Q1156374|lt=Yame District, Fukuoka|1=Yame District, Fukuoka}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. Including the surrounding moat and embankment, its total outer diameter reaches about 55 meters, making it the largest kofun in the area. The tomb was accidentally discovered in 1970 during orchard development.<ref name=genchi>On-site explanatory plaque.</ref>
The burial chamber is a slightly bulging horizontal stone chamber built with stacked schist stones. Inside, a stone cist for placing the deceased was constructed. The back and side walls of the cist are adorned with red and green painted motifs, including circular patterns, concentric circles, triangular designs, {{ill|bipedal ring pattern|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bipedal ring pattern|qq=}}, and depictions of {{ill|quivers|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quivers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=Yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}}.<ref name=iwato>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.15.</ref> Although one-third of the stone chamber was destroyed during the kofun's discovery, it was later restored and preserved within a protective structure.<ref name=genchi />
A notable feature of Kōkadani Kofun is the presence of "bipedal ring pattern" ({{ill|双脚輪状文|ja|双脚輪状文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/双脚輪状文|1=Bipedal ring pattern|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117086855}}) among its decorations. This motif is rare, with only four known examples nationwide.<ref>Other examples include {{ill|Kamao Kofun|en|Kamao Kofun|WD=Q24866261|lt=Kamao Kofun|1=Kamao Kofun}} and {{ill|Yokoyama Kofun|ja|横山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Kumamoto Prefecture, and {{ill|Ōzuka Kofun|en|Ōzuka Kofun|WD=Q11572952|lt=Ōzuka Kofun|1=Ōzuka Kofun}} in Fukuoka Prefecture.</ref> Additionally, the presence of a stone cist, which is a characteristic of burial facilities in the Higo region, suggests a strong connection between the kofun's Interred_individual and the Higo area.<ref name=iwato />
Based on features such as the single-chamber stone chamber, Kōkadani Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century, slightly preceding the {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|lt=Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}}.<ref name=iwato />
Today, Kōkadani Kofun is maintained as a park alongside the adjacent {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}. The stone chamber is open to the public twice a year, in spring and autumn. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>

It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and is part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|qid=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}.
== Overview ==
Kōkadani Kofun is a two-tiered round Kofun with a diameter of approximately 39 meters and a height of about 7 meters. A replica of the chamber is displayed at the nearby {{ill|Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja|こふんぴあ広川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofunpia Hirokawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} facility.<ref>[https://www.town.hirokawa.fukuoka.jp/shisetsuguide/shisetsu_37.htm Kofunpia Hirokawa, Hirokawa Town]</ref>
